dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, something remarkable happened. Something I never thought I'd see.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} honored the agreement. They pulled back and left {quest_item} at the border as promised. {target_npc} presented the evidence to the settlement, and while not everyone is convinced, the raids have stopped. The {quest_focus} {quest_focus_is} safe, and even the doubters are having a hard time arguing with silence and full stomachs.
dialogueChunks.outro: You walked into the space between two enemies and found common ground. That takes a kind of bravery no weapon can match.

playerResponses.text: Peace built on understanding is worth any risk. I'm glad it held.
I hope it lasts. Take care of what you've built here.

dialogueChunks.intro: It's not the ending I hoped for, {player_name}. But it's better than I feared.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {quest_focus} {quest_focus_was} returned to the {enemy_type} in the end. It was the right thing to do. {target_npc} stood at the border and watched them leave, and I swear I saw something pass between them: not friendship exactly, but recognition. The raids have slowed, though not stopped entirely.
dialogueChunks.outro: You proved that seeing the enemy as more than a monster doesn't make you a traitor. It makes you human.

playerResponses.text: Understanding doesn't mean surrender. The settlement will learn that in time.
It was the right call. Give it time.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}... it ended in blood. Not the way I wanted, but the truth still matters.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The hardliners found the {quest_focus} before we could move them to safety. There was a confrontation, and {target_npc} was injured trying to intervene. The {enemy_type} attacked in force when they learned what happened. But in the aftermath, some of the fighters admitted they saw the {quest_focus} trying to protect our people during the chaos.
dialogueChunks.outro: You tried to bridge the impossible, and even though the bridge broke, some people crossed it in their hearts. That has to count for something.

playerResponses.text: The truth has a way of surviving violence. I'm sorry it came to this.
Not every story ends well. But the attempt was worth making.
